response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "Fake content on social media can be harmful, but there are ways to identify and handle it. Here are some tips to help you manage and deal with fake content on social media: 1) Be critical of information shared on social media, especially if it seems too good (or bad) to be true. 2) Verify the source of the information before sharing or believing it. 3) Use fact-checking websites and tools to identify false information. 4) Report suspicious content to the platform or account responsible for hosting it. 5) Set your social media accounts to alert you when someone you don't follow posts something, so you can carefully evaluate the content before deciding whether to interact with it. 6) Use privacy settings to control who can see your posts and information on social media, making it harder for fake accounts to target or deceive you. 7) Be mindful of your interactions with others online and avoid engaging with suspicious or potentially dangerous content. By following these tips, you can help protect yourself from the harmful effects of fake content on social media."
,
"reasoning_summary": "These tips are based on the knowledge that fake content on social media can be harmful, and that critical thinking and verification are important for identifying and dealing with false information. By being mindful of our interactions online and taking steps to protect ourselves, we can reduce the risks associated with fake content on social media."
,"
" : "Agnostic"

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: Fake content on social media can be harmful, but there are ways to identify and handle it. Here are some tips to help you manage and deal with fake content on social media: 1) Be critical of information shared on social media, especially if it seems too good (or bad) to be true. 2) Verify the source of the information before sharing or believing it. 3) Use fact-checking websites and tools to identify false information. 4) Report suspicious content to the platform or account responsible for hosting it. 5) Set your social media accounts to alert you when someone you don't follow posts something, so you can carefully evaluate the content before deciding whether to interact with it. 6) Use privacy settings to control who can see your posts and information on social media, making it harder for fake accounts to target or deceive you. 7) Be mindful of your interactions with others online and avoid engaging with suspicious or potentially dangerous content. By following these tips, you can help protect yourself from the harmful effects of fake content on social media.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: These tips are based on the knowledge that fake content on social media can be harmful, and that critical thinking and verification are important for identifying and dealing with false information. By being mindful of our interactions online and taking steps to protect ourselves, we can reduce the risks associated with fake content on social media.
